content.text: Answer four problems. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Prove the Chinese remainder theorem: Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with \(1\) and let \(I,J\) be ideals in \(R\) such that \(I+J=R\). Then \(IJ=I\cap J\) and there is a ring isomorphism \(R/IJ\cong(R/I)\times(R/J)\).

Prove that the ring \(\mathbb{Z}[\sqrt{-2}]=\{a+b\sqrt{-2}:a,b\in\mathbb{Z}\}\) is a Euclidean domain with respect to the field norm
\[
N:\mathbb{Z}[\sqrt{-2}]\to\mathbb{Z}_{\geq 0},\qquad N(a+b\sqrt{-2})=a^2+2b^2.
\]
Prove that each of the following is irreducible in the indicated ring.
Let \(F\) be a field and let \(V\) be a vector space over \(F\). Let \(S\subset V\) be a set which spans \(V\). Use Zorn’s lemma to prove that \(V\) has a basis which is contained in \(S\).
Determine the number of similarity classes of matrices \(A\in\mathrm{M}_5(\mathbb{C})\) satisfying \(A^3=0\). Give a representative for each similarity class in Jordan canonical form.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(L/K\) be a field extension of degree \(5\) and let \(f(X)\in K[X]\) have degree \(4\). Prove that if \(\alpha\in L\) is a root of \(f(X)\), then \(\alpha\in K\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a field extension \(L/K\) of degree \(4\), a polynomial \(f(X)\in K[X]\) of degree \(5\), and a root \(\alpha\) of \(f(X)\) such that \(\alpha\in L\) but \(\alpha\notin K\).
